Steps: All Tasks table: Sort menu > pick Due date, then pick Due date again
Expected: First pick sorts by due ascending; second pick flips to descending; Sort button label updates both times

Steps: Group by menu: switch Due > Assignee
Expected: List regroups under assignee headers; any previously collapsed groups come back expanded

Steps: Click a group header caret to collapse, click again to expand
Expected: Group collapses/expands; other groups unaffected

Steps: Switch layout table > kanban > cards > table
Expected: Each layout renders the same task set; no console errors

Steps: Type in the search box, then clear it
Expected: List narrows as you type; clearing restores the full list

Steps: Flip the My work / Company segment both ways
Expected: List narrows to your tasks and back; choice survives reload

Steps: Calendar layout: toggle month > week > month
Expected: Mode switches; any selected day clears on each toggle

Steps: Click prev/next arrows a few times in month and week mode
Expected: Anchor moves by month/week; day list follows

Steps: Click a day with tasks, then click the same day again
Expected: First click selects the day and lists its tasks; second click deselects

Steps: Home: click a date on the mini-calendar
Expected: Jumps to All Tasks calendar anchored on that date with the day pre-selected; URL becomes #/tasks/calendar/<date>

Steps: Open a task from the table; press j and k a few times; press Esc
Expected: Detail opens with URL #/task/<id>; j/k walk next/prev tasks (wraps at ends); Esc closes and URL returns to #/tasks

Steps: Sidebar: Home > All Tasks > Urgent > Today > a Team view > back to All Tasks
Expected: Every view renders; All Tasks always lands on the table layout regardless of the layout it was left in

Steps: Focus widget > Open (enters Execution order), then click another view in the sidebar
Expected: Sort exits back to Priority; Sort button label says Priority; Focus widget shows its normal state

Steps: Open New task page, then click the logo/Home
Expected: Form closes and Home renders (no stuck overlay)

Steps: Open the filter panel; toggle an assignee chip and a status chip
Expected: Panel opens; list narrows; active-filter count badge updates

Steps: Set a due-range filter (e.g. This week), then set it back to All
Expected: List narrows and restores

Steps: Tasks board: click a company chip in the top row, then All
Expected: List scopes to that company and back; app-wide company switcher unaffected

Steps: Save the current view; change filters+sort+group+layout; apply the saved view
Expected: Board restores exactly what was saved

Steps: Projects: open a folder
Expected: List scopes to the folder with a folder header; URL becomes #/folder/<id>

Steps: Set sort+group+filters+scope, reload the app
Expected: Everything restores; layout opens on table (by design)

Steps: Topbar company switcher: change company
Expected: Every list, sidebar badge, and head card refreshes to the new company

Steps: Admin: View as > Worker, then back
Expected: Chrome and views re-render under the previewed role and restore correctly

Steps: Calendar already on the current month with nothing selected: click Today
Expected: Nothing re-renders or flickers (previously caused a redundant re-render)

Steps: With no task open, press Esc
Expected: Nothing happens; no console errors (previously fired a redundant selection event)

Steps: Open a folder, then Clear filters
Expected: Folder scope clears AND the URL hash leaves #/folder/... (pre-C5 the hash went stale)

Steps: Open a folder, reload the app
Expected: Folder scope is restored deterministically (pre-C5 it depended on whether another setting was changed afterwards)

Steps: Apply a saved view that matches the current state exactly
Expected: No visible re-render or flicker (previously fired 4 unconditional events)

Steps: Keep DevTools console open for the entire pass
Expected: No [UiStatePolicy] unknown-field errors; no new errors of any kind
